steps.title: Find the median of the original dataset
steps.content:
The original data is 6, 6, 8, 8, 8, 10, 21, which contains 7 values.
For an odd count, the median is the middle value at position 4.
The 4th value is 8.

steps.title: Find which choice has the same median
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (6, 6, 8, 8, 10, 10): This list has 6 values (even count).
The median is the average of the 3rd and 4th values:
$$\frac{8 + 8}{2} = 8$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
